---
title: "IV Flowchart"
author: "Ian Mccarthy"
date: "Today"
output: html_document
---

This describes some standard IV tests and sensitivity analyses:

  
```{r, echo=FALSE}
DiagrammeR::grViz("

  digraph instrumental_variables {

    # add node statements
    node [shape = box]
    A [label = 'So you want to do IV, eh?']
  
    node [shape = box]
    a [label = 'how big is the problem?']
    b [label = 'specification tests']
    c [label = 'sensitivity']
    
    node [shape = box]
    c1 [label = 'IV is biased!']
    c2 [label = 'what about outliers?']
    
    
    node [shape = box]
    a1  [label = 
          <
          Oster (2017)<br ALIGN='LEFT'/>          
          Test of how sensitive<br ALIGN='LEFT'/>
          estimates are to increasing<br ALIGN='LEFT'/>
          amounts of selection on unobservables.<br ALIGN='LEFT'/>
          In other words, how big of a problem<br ALIGN='LEFT'/>
          must selection on unobservables<br ALIGN='LEFT'/>
          be in order to make us question<br ALIGN='LEFT'/>
          the initial results?<br ALIGN='LEFT'/>
          psacalc in Stata<br ALIGN='LEFT'/>
          >
        ]
    c21 [label = <Cohen et al (2011), robivreg in Stata>]
    c12 [label = <Andrews and Armstrong (2017)<br ALIGN='LEFT'/> 
                  aaniv in Stata<br ALIGN='LEFT'/> >]
    
    
    # add edge statements
    edge [color = grey, arrowhead = none, arrowtail = none]
    A -> a; A -> b; A -> c; a -> a1; 
    c -> c1; c -> c2; c2 -> c21;
    
    
    
    # add graph statement
    graph [nodesep = 0.1]
  
  }
      
")

```
